verdict_reason: The verse's specific care — refusing the moralistic reading and preserving the structural-causal one — is itself the anti-trivialisation move. The transliterated Sanskrit (cetas, samādhi, bhoga-aiśvarya-prasakti, apahṛta, vyavasāyātmikā buddhi) is preserved load-bearing. The original meaning is preserved in literal_meaning and traditional_meaning_consensus. Both Shankara's and Ramanuja's readings are recorded without conflation. The verse does not claim the Sanskrit is 'really about' debugger sessions; the Tokio scenario is one operational analog of the broader cognitive-state claim. No trivialisation.
